children.heading: Designation of lead agency
children.label: b Designation of lead agency
children.text: The Secretary of Energy (in this section referred to as the “Secretary”), acting through the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy, shall act as the lead Federal agency for coordinating and disseminating information on existing Federal programs and assistance that may be used to help initiate, develop, and finance energy efficiency, renewable energy, and energy retrofitting projects for schools.

in consultation and coordination with the appropriate Federal agencies, carry out a review of existing programs and financing mechanisms (including revolving loan funds and loan guarantees) available in or from the Department of Agriculture, the Department of Energy, the Department of Education, the Department of the Treasury, the Internal Revenue Service, the Environmental Protection Agency, and other appropriate Federal agencies with jurisdiction over energy financing and facilitation that are currently used or may be used to help initiate, develop, and finance energy efficiency, renewable energy, and energy retrofitting projects for schools;

children.label: A
children.text: to use existing Federal opportunities more effectively; and

children.label: B
children.text: to form partnerships with Governors, State energy programs, local educational, financial, and energy officials, State and local government officials, nonprofit organizations, and other appropriate entities, to support the initiation of the projects;

children.label: A
children.text: to increase the energy efficiency of buildings or facilities;

children.label: B
children.text: to install systems that individually generate energy from renewable energy resources;

children.label: C
children.text: to establish partnerships to leverage economies of scale and additional financing mechanisms available to larger clean energy initiatives; or

children.label: i
children.text: the maintenance of health, environmental quality, and safety in schools, including the ambient air quality, through energy efficiency, renewable energy, and energy retrofit projects; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the achievement of expected energy savings and renewable energy production through proper operations and maintenance practices;

develop and maintain a single online resource website with contact information for relevant technical assistance and support staff in the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy for States, local educational agencies, and schools to effectively access and use Federal opportunities and assistance described in paragraph (1) to develop energy efficiency, renewable energy, and energy retrofitting projects; and

children.label: A
children.text: have successfully implemented energy efficiency, renewable energy, and energy retrofitting projects; and

children.label: B
children.text: are willing to serve as resources for other local educational agencies and schools to assist initiation of similar efforts.

children.heading: Report
children.label: d Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report describing the implementation of this section.December 27, 2020
